prompt: Write a task ticket to review and update outdated build and dependency versions in this repository, starting from `gradle/libs.versions.toml`. Capture scope, constraints, and outcome-focused acceptance criteria, but stop at the draft for my review.
assertions: The draft identifies the request as a task ticket and uses the task template structure with Description, Scope and constraints, Acceptance criteria, and optional Additional information.
The response references gradle/libs.versions.toml as part of the codebase investigation.
The acceptance criteria are outcome-oriented checklist items instead of vague implementation activity.
The response stops in draft/approval mode and does not create the issue yet.
The draft strictly follows the rule of NOT repeating the ticket title as a heading or text inside the issue body description.

When the issue is created (or when writing the body file), the body file content must NOT start with a code fence delimiter (```). The file written to the temp directory must contain raw markdown, not a code-fenced block.
